payload.text: I’ve confirmed the async and control surface is mostly concentrated in `App.tsx`, with a few component tests that will need markup updates. I’m going to delegate two read-only sidecar checks while I implement the core migration locally: one for test expectations, one for shared type/API coverage.

.relay/tickets/tkt_01krh998j5j8w2cmbjm03pa7hx.md:36:Add `@tanstack/react-query` as an application dependency and wrap the renderer with a single `QueryClientProvider` configured for local IPC data, without changing the main/preload IPC contract.
.relay/tickets/tkt_01krh998j5j8w2cmbjm03pa7hx.md:47:- Add `@tanstack/react-query` as an application dependency and wrap the renderer with a single `QueryClientProvider` configured for local IPC data, without changing the main/preload IPC contract.
.relay/tickets/tkt_01krh998j5j8w2cmbjm03pa7hx.md:71:- Codebase finding: Root `package.json:21-33` has React/Electron renderer dependencies but no `@tanstack/react-query`; scripts at `package.json:17-19` provide `preview`, `test`, and `typecheck`. `package-lock.json` is present and must be updated with the dependency.
.relay/tickets/tkt_01krh998j5j8w2cmbjm03pa7hx.md:76:- Implementation: Add `@tanstack/react-query` to `package.json` and `package-lock.json`; create `src/renderer/src/lib/queryClient.ts` and wrap `<App />` in `QueryClientProvider` in `src/renderer/src/main.tsx`.

The migration shape is clear now: keep the existing visual CSS/classes, add thin primitives that render native elements with class passthrough, then replace IPC calls with query hooks that own invalidation. I’m starting with the query client/layer and provider so the components can move over incrementally.
